title: FDRS_en
theory_name: Flattened Dimensional Reconstructive Theory II (FDRS II) — Lean 4 formalization

primitives.description: FDRS-admissible finite chain complex encoded by a dimension list (dim) and per-boundary-operator singular value lists (sing), with five admissibility constraints.
Sum of squares of a rational list; represents the total singular value energy of a boundary operator.
Sum of a list of naturals; used to compute the total chain group dimension of a complex.
Flattens a list of lists; aggregates all singular values across all boundary operators.
primitives.evidence: def flatten {α : Type} : List (List α) → List α

axioms.name: FDRS admissibility conditions (Ch_FDRS)
axioms.description: Five structural constraints (nonempty dims, one boundary operator per adjacent pair, shape match, injectivity via dim C_{i+1} ≤ dim C_i, strictly positive singular values) placing the complex in Ch_FDRS(ℝ).
axioms.evidence: Five admissibility constraints ensure the complex lies in Ch_FDRS(ℝ)

axioms.name: Single-step dimension reduction Δ₁ (dimension evolution)
axioms.formula: Δ₁(dim) folds top group C_n into coker ∂_n: new dim at n-1 = dim C_{n-1} - dim C_n
axioms.description: Definition 3.5: eliminates the top-dimensional chain group and replaces the next level with the cokernel dimension.
axioms.evidence: def delta1Dim (dim : List Nat) : List Nat :=

axioms.name: Single-step dimension reduction Δ₁ (singular value evolution)
axioms.formula: Δ₁(sing) discards the singular values of the top boundary operator ∂_n
axioms.description: Companion evolution rule to Δ₁ on singular value data: drop the last singular value list.
axioms.evidence: def delta1Sing (sing : List (List Rat)) : List (List Rat) :=

axioms.name: k-step dimension reduction Δ_k (dimensions)
axioms.description: Iterated application of delta1Dim k times.
axioms.evidence: def deltaKDim (dim : List Nat) (k : Nat) : List Nat :=

axioms.name: k-step dimension reduction Δ_k (singular values)
axioms.description: Iterated application of delta1Sing k times.
axioms.evidence: def deltaKSing (sing : List (List Rat)) (k : Nat) : List (List Rat) :=

axioms.name: Single-step information distortion operator D(Δ₁) (linear mode)
axioms.formula: D_lin(Δ₁(H)) = sumSq(top ∂_n singular values) / sumNat(dim H)
axioms.description: Definition 3.1: fraction of total information energy lost in one reduction step, normalized by total chain group dimension.

axioms.name: Discarded energy over k steps (sumTopKSq)
axioms.description: Total discarded singular value energy of the top k boundary operators.
axioms.evidence: def sumTopKSq (sing : List (List Rat)) (k : Nat) : Rat :=

axioms.name: Cumulative distortion D(Δ_k)
axioms.description: Cumulative information distortion of a k-step reduction: total discarded energy normalized by total dimension.

axioms.name: Spectral-mode distortion operator D_spec(Δ₁)
axioms.description: In spectral observation mode, distortion is normalized by total singular value energy instead of total dimension; the asymmetry with D_lin sources the R-Δ non-commutativity.

theorems.name: Theorem 5.1: Energy Conservation
theorems.statement: distortionK(H,k) + sumSq(flatten(Δ_k(H.sing)))/sumNat(H.dim) = sumSq(flatten(H.sing))/sumNat(H.dim): discarded energy exactly accounts for distortion, no residual error.
theorems.description: Main theorem: cumulative distortion plus normalized retained energy equals total normalized energy of the original complex.

theorems.name: Theorem 5.2: R-Δ Non-commutativity Theorem
theorems.statement: D_spec(Δ₁(H)) - D_lin(Δ₁(H)) = σ_top² · (1/E_total - 1/D_total): the gap between mode-switch-then-reduce and reduce-then-mode-switch is fixed by spectral data.
theorems.description: Quantifies order-dependence between the representation transformation operator R and the dimension meta-operator Δ.

theorems.name: Core Energy Lemma: energy partition
theorems.statement: sumSq(flatten(Δ_k(sing))) + sumTopKSq(sing,k) = sumSq(flatten(sing)): retained plus discarded singular value energy equals total energy.
theorems.description: Un-normalized backbone of Theorem 5.1, proved via take/drop splitting of the singular value lists.

theorems.name: Corollary: D(Δ_k) definitional identity
theorems.statement: distortionK H k = sumTopKSq H.sing k / sumNat H.dim holds by construction (rfl).
theorems.description: Records that the original definitional identity of cumulative distortion remains valid.

theorems.name: Bridge Lemma: Δ_k as List.take
theorems.statement: deltaKSing sing k = sing.take (sing.length - k), bridging the reduction operator to standard list operations without Mathlib.
theorems.description: Key bridge using Init's List.drop_reverse; enables the energy partition proof.

theorems.name: Lemma: Δ_k drops the last k entries
theorems.description: Characterizes k-step singular value reduction as dropping the last k boundary operators.

theorems.name: Lemma: Δ₁ as reverse-tail-reverse
theorems.description: Connects the pattern-match definition of Δ₁ to standard list tail operations.

theorems.name: List lemma: drop (n+1) = tail of drop n
theorems.statement: l.drop (n + 1) = (l.drop n).tail, proved from Init without Mathlib.
theorems.description: Auxiliary list lemma used in deltaKSing_spec.
theorems.evidence: theorem drop_succ {α : Type} (n : Nat) (l : List α) : l.drop (n + 1) = (l.drop n).tail := by

theorems.name: List lemma: flatten distributes over ++
theorems.statement: flatten (l₁ ++ l₂) = flatten l₁ ++ flatten l₂.
theorems.description: Distribution of flatten over concatenation, used in energy partition.
theorems.evidence: theorem flatten_append {α : Type} (l₁ l₂ : List (List α)) :

theorems.name: List lemma: sumSq additive over ++
theorems.statement: sumSq (l₁ ++ l₂) = sumSq l₁ + sumSq l₂.
theorems.description: Additivity of singular value energy over concatenation.

theorems.name: Rational lemma: (a+b)/c = a/c + b/c
theorems.statement: Additive distribution of rational division over addition.
theorems.description: Arithmetic helper for combining fractions in energy_conservation.
theorems.evidence: theorem Rat.add_div (a b c : Rat) : (a + b) / c = a / c + b / c := by

theorems.name: Rational lemma: a*(b-c) = a*b - a*c
theorems.statement: Left distributivity of rational multiplication over subtraction.
theorems.description: Arithmetic helper closing the R-Δ non-commutativity proof.
theorems.evidence: theorem Rat.mul_sub (a b c : Rat) : a * (b - c) = a * b - a * c := by

the SVD energy ratio (which would be a tautology), but that this ratio is verified

notes: Source is English-annotated Lean 4 code (not prose): defs mapped to axioms, theorems/lemmas to theorems, structure FDRSComplex plus its five field constraints bundled as one admissibility axiom.
